problem_statement: Checked-baggage pricing uses the wrong haul classification: flights under 6 hours are being treated as long-haul and charged €70, while flights 6 hours or longer are being treated as short-haul and charged €35.
goal: Fix the haul classification logic so that checked-baggage pricing applies €35 for flights shorter than 6 hours and €70 for flights of 6 hours or longer.
acceptance_criteria: A checked bag on a flight shorter than 6 hours is priced at 35.00 when no explicit price is provided.
A checked bag on a flight of 6 hours or longer is priced at 70.00 when no explicit price is provided.
The haul classification helper returns the expected boolean for the 6-hour threshold.
constraints: Keep the change localized to the booking service logic unless tests reveal a second dependent location.
Preserve existing explicit baggage pricing behavior, only affecting the default price path driven by haul classification.
unknowns: Whether any tests currently encode the inverted threshold and need updating.
Whether the haul classification helper is used anywhere else for pricing or only for baggage fees.
repo_summary: Backend-focused Python service with booking, flight, and testing services; issue points directly to backend/app/services/booking_service.py and helper _is_long_haul.

targeted_symbols.reason: Directly named in the issue as the likely source of the inverted 6-hour threshold used to determine baggage fee defaults.

localization_rationale: The issue explicitly identifies backend/app/services/booking_service.py and the helper _is_long_haul as the affected area.
The symptom is a reversed threshold at 6 hours, which strongly suggests a single boolean helper or conditional in booking service rather than a broader pricing system defect.
The most likely companion test area is backend/tests/services/test_booking_service.py, where the default baggage pricing behavior should be asserted or corrected.
